title: pseudognaphalium helleri
intro: Pseudognaphalium helleri, also known as Heller's cudweed or Heller's rabbit-tobacco, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the daisy family, Asteraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Pseudognaphalium helleri include reducing inflammation, soothing digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Pseudognaphalium helleri is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in salves and ointments to topical applications.
